_comment: Tight-token base policy (/css-wisdom tight-token-strategy): forbid Tailwind's default numeric freedom-scale + default color palette. ALLOW semantic tokens (hsp-/vsp-/…), arbitrary values (w-[30px], bg-[hsl(...)]), fractions (w-1/2, top-1/2, size-1/2), keywords (w-full, max-w-prose), and 0. SCOPE LIMITATION: this list bans only fraction-FREE prefixes (spacing + colors). Fraction-capable prefixes — sizing (w/h/size/basis/min-w/max-w/min-h/max-h) and positioning (inset/top/right/bottom/left/start/end) — are intentionally NOT banned: the lint tool (@takazudo/zudo-design-token-lint) strips everything after '/' as an opacity modifier (rules.js), so banning e.g. w-{n} or top-{n} would also false-positive the valid fractions w-1/2 / top-1/2. Opacity only applies to color utilities in Tailwind, so the proper fix is upstream (only strip /N opacity for color rules); tracked there + zudolab/zudo-css-wisdom#112. Until that lands, the freedom-scale ban for sizing/positioning is enforced by review, not lint.
suggestionSuffix: use hsp-*/vsp-* tokens, an arbitrary value like w-[30px], a fraction like w-1/2, or a semantic token
